No, it doesn't. LU-14631 exists only with limits grater than 1T, while sanity-quota_1d uses 10,12 and 20MB.

parents.subject: LU-14613 tests: fix sanity/60f on a local setup

commit.subject: LU-14631 tests: write hung with quota limit 53T
commit.message:
LU-14631 tests: write hung with quota limit 53T

Check that write in sanity-quota_1e returns EDQUOT
and doesn't hung when global_limit set to 53T.

HPE-bug-id: LUS-9891
Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-quota env=ONLY="1e"
Change-Id: I904d68ef3b598682c6930a92b3aa774096b6840a
Signed-off-by: Sergey Cheremencev <sergey.cheremencev@hpe.com>
Reviewed-by: Alexander Boyko <c17825@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Shaun Tancheff <stancheff@cray.com>
Tested-by: Elena Gryaznova <c17455@cray.com>
